fault_hypothesis: The expiry check on line 61 uses strict greater-than (`now > effective`) instead of greater-than-or-equal (`now >= effective`), so when `now == effective` (exact boundary) the entry is incorrectly returned as valid instead of expired.

constraints.intent: When now equals effective, the function must return None (expired); the strict > check fails this at the boundary.
Capture the relationship between now and effective immediately after effective is computed, to verify the boundary condition is handled.
In the else branch (entry not expired), now must be strictly less than effective; equality should have triggered expiry.
The payload should only be returned when now is strictly less than the effective TTL.
